criteria.label: Distinguish code vs Markdown cells and run cells with the keyboard.
criteria.example: `Esc → M` converts the active cell to Markdown; `Esc → Y` flips it back to code. Run with `Shift+Enter` (run + next), `Ctrl+Enter` (run, stay), `Alt+Enter` (run + insert).

criteria.label: Explain that the kernel holds session state across cells.
criteria.example: Cell 1: `client = OpenAI()`. Cell 7: `client.chat.completions.create(...)`. The kernel keeps `client` alive between runs — that's why you can split a workflow across many cells without re-creating objects.

criteria.label: Read In [n]/Out[n] correctly and diagnose hidden-state bugs.
criteria.example: If a notebook's cell *positions* read 1→2→3 but the labels say `In [5]`, `In [3]`, `In [7]`, the cells were run out of order. Variables in the kernel reflect that order — *not* the page order. Visually scanning labels reveals stale-state risk.

criteria.label: Use Restart & Run All to guarantee reproducibility.
criteria.example: Before sharing a notebook: Kernel → Restart & Run All. If it doesn't run cleanly end-to-end in a fresh kernel, your conclusions depend on hidden state — fix it before publishing.
